title: Challenges Grow for Green Energy Projects in the UK and Europe
subtitle: From electric vehicle trade rules to steel industry upgrades, major green energy plans are facing unexpected delays.

summary.en: The car industry is asking the European Commission to delay new rules on electric vehicle trade. Manufacturers argue they cannot meet strict local production targets for batteries by 2027. Meanwhile, Tata Steel faces a potential eight-month delay for a major new electric furnace project in Wales. These setbacks highlight the difficulty of building the necessary infrastructure for a greener economy. Both sectors are now calling for urgent government support to keep their environmental goals on track.
summary.zh: 汽車產業正要求歐盟執委會延後實施電動車貿易新規定，製造商認為他們無法在二〇二七年前達成嚴格的電池在地生產目標。與此同時，塔塔鋼鐵在威爾斯的一項大型新型電爐計畫可能面臨八個月的延誤。這些挫折凸顯了為綠色經濟建立必要基礎設施的困難。目前這兩個產業皆呼籲政府提供緊急支援，以確保其環保目標能持續推進。

paragraphs.en: Major industries in the UK and Europe are facing significant hurdles as they try to transition to greener technologies. Recent reports show that both the automotive and steel sectors are struggling with infrastructure and supply chain issues, raising questions about whether current environmental targets can be met on time.
paragraphs.zh: 英國與歐洲的主要產業在嘗試轉型為更環保的技術時，正面臨重大阻礙。近期報告顯示，汽車與鋼鐵產業皆在基礎設施與供應鏈問題上陷入困境，這引發了外界對於現行環保目標能否如期達成的質疑。

paragraphs.en: In the automotive sector, car manufacturers are urging the European Commission to delay new rules regarding electric vehicle (EV) trade. Under the current Brexit deal, vehicles sold between the UK and the EU must meet strict "rules of origin" to avoid tariffs—which are taxes on imported goods. By January 1, 2027, a large percentage of a car’s value, as well as its battery components, must be produced within Europe to qualify for tariff-free trade.
paragraphs.zh: 在汽車產業方面，車廠正敦促歐盟執委會（European Commission）延後實施關於電動車貿易的新規定。根據目前的英國脫歐協議，英國與歐盟之間銷售的車輛必須符合嚴格的「原產地規則」，以避免被課徵關稅（即針對進口商品所徵收的稅款）。到了二〇二七年一月一日，汽車價值與電池組件必須有很大比例是在歐洲境內生產，才能享有免關稅貿易的資格。

paragraphs.en: However, industry leaders say these goals are no longer realistic. According to The Guardian, the European Automobile Manufacturers’ Association (ACEA) estimated that only about 20% of batteries will be made in the EU by the 2027 deadline. This is far below the original expectations. Sigrid de Vries, the director general of ACEA, stated that the development of battery production in Europe is moving too slowly. Manufacturers point to several reasons for this, including the high cost of production compared to China and a global shortage of raw materials like lithium.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，產業領袖表示這些目標已不再切合實際。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，歐洲汽車製造商協會（ACEA）估計，到了二〇二七年的期限，僅有約百分之二十的電池會在歐盟境內製造，這遠低於最初的預期。歐洲汽車製造商協會秘書長德弗里斯（Sigrid de Vries）指出，歐洲電池生產的發展速度過於緩慢。製造商將此歸咎於多項原因，包括相較於中國的高昂生產成本，以及鋰等原材料的全球性短缺。

paragraphs.en: Mike Hawes, chief executive of the Society of Motor Manufacturers and Traders (SMMT), noted that the current supply chains are not ready to meet these strict requirements. He warned that without a change in policy, the industry could face "self-defeating tariffs" on the very vehicles that consumers are being encouraged to buy. The industry is now asking for a second delay to these rules to allow more time for domestic battery manufacturing to grow.
paragraphs.zh: 英國汽車製造商與貿易商協會（SMMT）執行長霍斯（Mike Hawes）指出，目前的供應鏈尚未準備好滿足這些嚴格要求。他警告，若政策不做出調整，產業可能會對消費者被鼓勵購買的車輛面臨「自我挫敗的關稅」。目前業界正要求針對這些規定進行第二次延期，以爭取更多時間讓國內電池製造業發展。

paragraphs.en: At the same time, the steel industry is facing its own set of challenges. Tata Steel has warned that its new £1.25 billion electric arc furnace in Port Talbot, Wales, may be delayed by six to eight months. This project is a key part of the company’s plan to move away from traditional, high-pollution blast furnaces. The new technology is designed to be much cleaner, but it requires a massive amount of electricity to operate.
paragraphs.zh: 與此同時，鋼鐵產業也面臨著自身的挑戰。塔塔鋼鐵（Tata Steel）警告，其位於威爾斯塔爾伯特港（Port Talbot）價值十二點五億英鎊的新型電弧爐計畫，可能會延宕六至八個月。此計畫是該公司擺脫傳統高污染高爐計畫的核心部分。這項新技術旨在大幅降低污染，但運作時需要消耗極為龐大的電力。

paragraphs.en: According to BBC Business, the delay is caused by problems with electrical connectivity. Tata Steel is working with the National Grid—the company that manages the country's electricity system—to upgrade the infrastructure needed to power the new furnace. However, the National Grid has reported that its own project to install new cables and substations is behind schedule due to difficult ground conditions and planning requirements. Koushik Chatterjee, an executive director at Tata Steel, told investors that securing access to high-power electricity is critical for the project, and the company is currently in talks with the UK government to find a solution.
paragraphs.zh: 根據英國廣播公司商業新聞（BBC Business）報導，延誤原因是電力連接問題。塔塔鋼鐵正與負責管理國家電力系統的國家電網公司（National Grid）合作，升級為新熔爐供電所需的基礎設施。然而，國家電網公司表示，由於地質條件困難及規劃要求，其安裝新電纜與變電站的計畫進度落後。塔塔鋼鐵執行董事查特吉（Koushik Chatterjee）向投資人表示，確保取得高功率電力對該計畫至關重要，公司目前正與英國政府商討解決方案。

paragraphs.en: These two situations highlight the complex reality of moving toward a greener economy. While governments and companies have set ambitious goals to reduce carbon emissions, the physical work required to reach those goals is proving to be both expensive and time-consuming. In the case of EVs, the challenge is building a local supply chain that can compete with international markets. In the case of the steel industry, the challenge is upgrading national infrastructure to support new, cleaner technology.
paragraphs.zh: 這兩起案例凸顯了邁向綠色經濟過程中的複雜現實。儘管政府與企業已設定了雄心勃勃的減碳目標，但要達成這些目標所需的實質工作，已被證明既昂貴又耗時。以電動車為例，挑戰在於建立能與國際市場競爭的在地供應鏈；以鋼鐵業為例，挑戰則在於升級國家基礎設施以支援更新、更乾淨的技術。

paragraphs.en: Experts suggest that these setbacks are not just isolated incidents but part of a broader struggle. As industries attempt to shift their operations, they are finding that the necessary support systems—such as stable energy supplies and local manufacturing hubs—are not yet fully developed. For now, both the car and steel industries are looking to policymakers for help. Whether through adjusting trade rules or speeding up infrastructure projects, the message from business leaders is clear: without more flexibility and support, the path to a greener future will be much harder to navigate than originally expected.
paragraphs.zh: 專家認為，這些挫折並非單一事件，而是更廣泛困境的一部分。隨著產業試圖轉型營運模式，他們發現必要的支援系統（如穩定的能源供應與在地製造中心）尚未完全發展成熟。目前，汽車與鋼鐵產業皆寄望政策制定者提供協助。無論是透過調整貿易規則還是加速基礎設施工程，企業領袖傳達的訊息很明確：若缺乏更多的彈性與支持，通往綠色未來的道路將比預期更加艱難。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Problems or difficulties that must be overcome.
keyTerms.noteZh: 原意指田徑比賽中的「跨欄」，這裡引申為企業面臨的困難。文中：Major industries in the UK and Europe are facing significant hurdles as they try to transition to greener technologies.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Happening or being finished later than originally planned.
keyTerms.noteZh: 雖然字面意思簡單，但這是描述專案管理時非常道地的用法。文中：However, the National Grid has reported that its own project to install new cables and substations is behind schedule due to difficult ground conditions and planning requirements.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To deal with a complicated or difficult situation.
keyTerms.noteZh: 原意指導航或駕駛船隻，這裡用來比喻處理困難的過程。文中：without more flexibility and support, the path to a greener future will be much harder to navigate than originally expected.

sources.name: The Guardian Business
sources.title: Car industry pressing EU for further delay to Brexit EV tariffs

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: Tata Steel says new £1.25bn furnace may be delayed due to electrical issue

previousArticles.title: UK Faces Pressure Over Electric Vehicle Rules and Carbon Goals
previousArticles.summaryEn: New analysis reveals that recent changes to UK electric vehicle rules may cause a significant rise in carbon emissions. The government is facing pressure from both car manufacturers and environmental groups regarding future policy. While carmakers argue that current targets are too difficult to meet, environmentalists warn that weakening the rules will hurt the environment. The charging industry also fears that fewer electric cars will threaten their business investments. The government has promised to review these rules again by early 2027.

previousArticles.title: The UK's Electric Vehicle Debate: A New Challenge for Green Energy
previousArticles.summaryEn: The United Kingdom is facing a difficult debate over its electric vehicle goals. New data shows that recent changes to government rules could lead to 17 million extra tonnes of carbon dioxide. Car makers argue that current targets are too difficult to reach in the current market. Meanwhile, charging companies warn that changing the rules again will hurt their investments. The government plans to review these policies again by early 2027.

quiz.question: What is the primary reason the European Automobile Manufacturers’ Association (ACEA) believes the 2027 EV trade rules are currently unrealistic?

options.A: The UK government has refused to cooperate with the European Commission.
options.B: The production of batteries within the EU is not scaling up as quickly as originally expected.
options.C: Consumers are no longer interested in purchasing electric vehicles.
options.D: The cost of shipping finished cars from China to Europe has become too expensive.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，ACEA 指出預計到 2027 年時，歐盟境內生產的電池比例僅佔約 20%，遠低於預期，且電池生產發展過慢。選項 A 文中未提及。選項 C 與文章內容相反，文中提到消費者被鼓勵購買電動車。選項 D 雖提到生產成本高，但並未提及運費是導致規則不切實際的主要原因。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the 'self-defeating tariffs' mentioned by Mike Hawes?

options.A: They are taxes designed to punish companies that produce too many batteries.
options.B: They are intended to encourage consumers to stop buying electric vehicles.
options.C: They would penalize the very industry they were originally meant to protect.
options.D: They are a result of the steel industry's failure to upgrade its furnaces.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第四段，Hawes 提到這些關稅會對「消費者被鼓勵購買的車輛」徵稅，這與推廣電動車的目標相違背，因此稱為「自我挫敗」。選項 A 不正確，關稅是針對未達產地比例的車輛。選項 B 與政策目標相反。選項 D 是關於鋼鐵業的問題，與汽車業的關稅無直接關聯。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'navigate' mean in the context of the sentence: 'the path to a greener future will be much harder to navigate than originally expected'?

options.A: To steer a ship or vehicle through a specific route.
options.B: To successfully deal with or manage a complicated situation.
options.C: To create a map for future environmental regulations.
options.D: To travel across a physical distance to reach a destination.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此語境下，「navigate」並非指物理上的導航或駕駛，而是比喻處理或應對複雜的過程與挑戰。選項 A 是該詞的字面義，在此不適用。選項 C 與地圖繪製無關。選項 D 雖然與旅行有關，但文章討論的是產業轉型過程的困難，而非地理移動。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the central message of the article?

options.A: The UK and European governments have successfully met all their environmental goals.
options.B: The automotive and steel industries are struggling because they refuse to adopt new technologies.
options.C: Transitioning to a greener economy is proving difficult due to infrastructure and supply chain constraints.
options.D: The only way to achieve environmental targets is to remove all trade tariffs between the UK and the EU.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章透過汽車業的電池供應鏈問題與鋼鐵業的電力基礎設施問題，說明了邁向綠色經濟的過程中，實體基礎設施與供應鏈的發展速度跟不上政策目標。選項 A 與文意完全相反。選項 B 錯誤，因為產業並非拒絕轉型，而是面臨實務困難。選項 D 過度簡化，調整關稅只是解決方案之一，並非唯一途徑。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
